objective: Every Computexchange facet reaches 5/5 through reproducible product evidence, not implementation claims.

scoring.rule: A facet is 5/5 only when every prerequisite and outcome gate is proven. Harnesses and scaffolds are prerequisites, not outcome credit. External gates cannot be satisfied by local simulations.

facets.definition_of_5: Every executable quote is independently evidenced, survives worst-case attempts and remedies, moves money idempotently, beats a named alternative on the chosen wedge, and yields reconciled positive contribution margin plus credible supplier value.

gates.acceptance: Quote-derived settlement is labeled as revenue, never execution cost, and cannot mutate catalogue prices.
An idempotent per-job projection preserves unknowns as null and derives liability, refunds, billed amount, actual processor fee allocation, and contribution margin only from persisted facts.
Submit and merge persist exact input/output units; execution and verification work are sourced; multi-job processor fees allocate exactly and idempotently with no guessed remainder.

A versioned frozen plan fails closed, separates supplier payout from buyer safety fees and SLA premium, prices partial completion and bounded extra accepted work, persists atomically, resists tampering, and permits only one primary/hedge money winner.

A historical collection fact is admitted only from an exact succeeded PaymentIntent and recorded once and immutably across standalone-job and batch sources; every supplier provider call has an immutable integer-cent reservation against that causal fact or a finite operator-declared subsidy pool. Unfunded debt leaves the bounded due page as awaiting_funding and only an exact cash fact or subsidy re-arms it. Definitely-unsent failures become ready, while ambiguous or stale calls remain sticky outcome_unknown, retry only the same key inside a conservative idempotency window, cannot be admin-rearmed, and become or remain reversal_required through clawback. Admin owed/cash views require a durable cash_moved operation; manual export is synced but non-cash; and a server-owned payout-hold floor applies. This call-side theorem does not prove that collections remain unrefunded or uncharged-back, provider lookup/reversal recovery, or that processor cash is currently available.

Every six-decimal supplier liability has one explicit deterministic minor-unit settlement policy: sub-cent value is carried without silent loss, each cash-proved released liability reconciles exactly to cash sent plus durable remainder, provider rounding is reflected in admission-margin reserves, and zero, sub-cent, unfunded, retry, concurrency, or ambiguous-response rows cannot abort a sweep, starve later payable liabilities, or erase owed value.

Every newly admitted subsidy-fund and payout-subsidy authorization is transactionally bound one-to-one to an append-only typed action carrying the live credential principal, authentication mode, honest attribution scope, normalized exact-request SHA-256, target, integer cents, currency, reason, and natural correlation reference. Passkey session/credential and break-glass key revocation are rechecked inside the money transaction; exact retries preserve the first actor and conflicting or forged/mismatched bindings fail without mutation. The bounded keyset review is no-store and omits treasury references, arbitrary detail, credential material, and key/session hashes. Pre-provenance rows remain visibly legacy_unattributed and fail closed for new authority. This proves credential-level attribution, not the human holding a shared key, external cash, DB-superuser resistance, or WORM retention.

No executable job can fall below the configured contribution-margin floor after every attempted retry, losing hedge, verification/dispute path, partial completion, processor fee, reversal, refund, and SLA remedy is charged to the job exactly once.
gates.next_action: Add immutable attempt/economic events, implement real reversal/refund/chargeback recovery, reserve processor/control/storage/support and bad-debt costs against the same cash boundary, reconcile operator-declared subsidy pools to external treasury cash, and fault-inject every terminal path against the frozen admission plan.

gates.acceptance: Every executable catalogue model/job/runtime quote is backed by current measured units and the active economic schedule; missing or stale evidence blocks execution instead of using aliases, seeds, or silent defaults.
gates.next_action: Create a coverage report joining the runtime matrix, catalogue, benchmark manifests, and schedule version; make submit fail any uncovered cell and populate physical cells only from signed benchmark bundles.

gates.acceptance: Charge, Connect transfer, retry, reversal, refund, webhook, and reconciliation are idempotent under crash and response-loss injection in Stripe test mode.
gates.next_action: Build a Stripe-test fault proxy and table-driven crash matrix around PaymentIntent, transfer, reversal, refund, and webhook boundaries; require ledger/Stripe convergence after replay.

gates.acceptance: A real low-value buyer charge, supplier Connect payout, refund or reversal, processor fee, and reconciliation complete with immutable provider and ledger references.
gates.next_action: After project policies and accounts are ready, authorize a capped live-money canary and archive redacted Stripe plus ledger evidence; no local mock can close this gate.

gates.acceptance: For the declared wedge workload, CX reports reproducible end-to-end buyer cost and time against OpenAI batch, a current GPU rental/serverless option, and local Ollama/MLX including queue, transfer, retries, verification, and operator time; the claim is narrowed or withdrawn when CX does not win.
gates.next_action: Choose one privacy or large-unified-memory batch workload, pin comparison manifests and current public prices, and publish a claim-safe comparison generated from raw artifacts.

gates.acceptance: At least ten independently controlled suppliers receive reconciled payouts whose net hourly value is measured against electricity, wear, taxes/fees disclosed by policy, and their stated reservation price.
gates.next_action: Instrument opt-in cost/value surveys and payout exports, then recruit the cohort only after live money and signed distribution are ready.

gates.acceptance: At least 100 paid jobs over 30 days have complete processor reconciliation and non-negative p50 and p95 contribution margin, with every negative outlier explained and bounded.
gates.next_action: Run the cohort after the live-money canary, export the economic-facts projection daily, and freeze the 30-day query plus raw provider references.

facets.definition_of_5: A durable verdict precedes settlement, receipts state exact coverage and execution identity, colluding substitution has a quantified detection bound, and an adversarial failure is financially reversed.

gates.acceptance: Audit selection is a stable server-secret HMAC decision unavailable to the worker and is made only after the result is durably committed; the proof injects a fresh non-exported secret only into control/test processes, and production or live-money startup refuses missing, undersized, or source-known fallback sampling secrets.

A missing artifact remains verifying and unpaid; after upload, verdict, completion counters, accepted telemetry, and ledger rows commit together exactly once, and penalty verdicts cannot release money.
Coverage and verdict are durable per delivered chunk; one checked chunk can never label unchecked chunks or the whole job fully verified.
The complete uploaded -> verifying -> accepted/rejected -> completed -> settled state machine recovers exactly once from a process crash at every database, object-store, and money-work boundary.
gates.next_action: Add named crash hooks at every transition, restart the control plane against the same database/object store, and compare one canonical terminal ledger for every injected boundary.

gates.acceptance: An independently verified receipt binds actual model weights, tokenizer, quantization/precision, runtime, kernels, configuration, agent release, and hardware class to an allowlisted manifest; smaller-model or lower-precision substitution is rejected even when all worker replicas collude.
gates.next_action: Define signed manifests and an independent challenge/fingerprint path over weights and runtime state, then add colluding smaller-model and precision-downgrade adversarial fixtures before physical confirmation.

gates.acceptance: The published audit policy gives a conservative detection probability and expected loss bound for burst cheating, long-con reputation attacks, supplier collusion, class substitution, and adaptive timing, validated by seeded simulations and physical adversaries.
gates.next_action: Build an adversary simulator driven by the real sampler/reputation rules, derive worst-case bounds, and verify them with planted workers rather than quoting average sampling rates.

gates.acceptance: A planted cheating physical worker is detected and rejected, its held or transferred test-mode funds are withheld/reversed, and the provider plus internal ledgers reconcile exactly.
gates.next_action: After execution identity and money recovery are green, run one disclosed adversarial worker through the physical fleet and archive the full verdict-to-reversal trace.

facets.definition_of_5: Independent buyers and suppliers can safely join, leave, recover, and transact through a truthful centrally coordinated exchange that survives control-plane failure and demonstrates sustained liquidity.

gates.acceptance: Buyer A cannot read, modify, onboard, or mint credentials for supplier B; ownership and ambiguous legacy rows fail closed in database integration tests.
CX rejects caller-supplied identity/tax fields, has no supplier tax-ID columns, and exposes Stripe Connect as the configured KYC provider; this does not prove a live hosted onboarding session.
A fresh supplier completes Stripe-hosted test onboarding, CX stores only Connect identifiers/status, webhook state converges after replay, and no identity/tax payload enters CX logs or storage.
gates.next_action: Configure a Stripe test Connect account, run a redacted browser/network/storage trace through onboarding, and replay the signed webhook sequence.

gates.acceptance: Signup, verification, login, API-key rotation, password recovery, session/device revocation, export, account deletion, and recovery from interrupted flows are non-skipped contract tests with rate limits and audit events.
gates.next_action: Write the lifecycle state machine and black-box contract suite, then implement the missing verification, recovery, export, and deletion transitions without relying on the future public site.

gates.acceptance: Leader leases and outbox-style work survive process death with no duplicate dispatch, settlement, payout, webhook, or stuck ownership.
gates.next_action: Introduce explicit leases/outboxes for singleton loops, run two control processes, and kill each owner at every claim/work acknowledgement boundary.

gates.acceptance: All product and marketing surfaces consistently describe the implemented central control-plane/Postgres scheduler and never call it peer-to-peer, brokerless in a way that implies decentralization, or multi-host clustered when those properties are absent.
gates.next_action: Add a claim vocabulary policy and repository scanner, inventory every P2P/decentralized/cluster statement, and fail CI on wording outside the proven topology.

gates.acceptance: Five unaffiliated buyers independently create accounts, obtain credentials, submit a supported real job, receive an honest receipt, and understand the charge without operator intervention.
gates.next_action: Recruit the cohort only after account lifecycle, distribution, policies, live money, and one supported wedge workload are ready; record intervention and abandonment.

gates.acceptance: Five paying buyers and ten independently controlled supplier machines sustain at least 95% eligible-job fill for 30 days, including measured supplier disconnect/rejoin, credential revocation, and replacement recovery.
gates.next_action: After activation, run a 30-day cohort dashboard with identity-distinct supply, fill denominator definitions, and scheduled recovery drills; do not count local processes as suppliers.

facets.definition_of_5: Canonical physical benchmarks demonstrate sustained, fault-tolerant, end-to-end advantages on a named buyer workload and continuously prevent regressions without cross-stack comparison drift.

gates.acceptance: A strict manifest pins workload/model hashes, tokenizer, precision, corpus, requested output, completed units, batch, runtime/toolchain, hardware, load/thermal/power, timing boundaries, sampling, and declared cross-lane differences.
gates.command: python3 -m unittest -v scripts/test_performance_proof.py && python3 scripts/performance_proof.py validate --baseline-manifest proof/performance/fixtures/synthetic-baseline.manifest.json --candidate-manifest proof/performance/fixtures/synthetic-candidate.manifest.json

gates.acceptance: A deterministic synthetic fixture proves evaluation of end-to-end output-token throughput, nearest-rank p95/p99, OOM, restart, disconnect, corrupt output, and cleanup while refusing to count synthetic data as physical evidence.

Every published comparison pins all manifest fields and includes queue, provisioning, transfer, verification, cleanup, and completed-output units; undeclared model/precision/engine/workload differences fail the comparison.
gates.next_action: Run the canonical manifests on the existing Mac, incoming Studio, and explicitly authorized CUDA references; sign raw observations and validate them before publishing any ratio.

gates.acceptance: Clean 30-minute physical runs record temperature, power, frequency, competing load, completed units, and recovery; concurrent builds or unrelated load invalidate thermal attribution.
gates.next_action: Reserve an otherwise-idle machine window, collect OS power/thermal/process telemetry beside benchmark observations, repeat three times, and compare recovery after cooldown.

gates.acceptance: Scheduled tests detect per-lane throughput, p95/p99, OOM, restart, disconnect/rejoin, corrupt output, cleanup, and provenance regressions against statistically declared thresholds.
gates.next_action: Add signed baseline promotion, noise bands, and CI/host scheduling around the existing evaluator; block promotion on any fault or provenance failure.

gates.acceptance: A named real buyer workload demonstrates a repeatable end-to-end latency, privacy, memory-capacity, or total-cost advantage against the strongest relevant paid and free substitute, with quality held constant.
gates.next_action: Select the wedge with a real design partner, freeze success criteria before running, and include OpenAI/serverless/rental plus local Ollama/MLX where applicable.

facets.definition_of_5: One authoritative matrix controls what can be advertised and scheduled; a buyer-demanded runtime and multiple physical Apple/CUDA lanes pass identity, recovery, fault, and soak promotion gates with no silent fallback.

gates.acceptance: An inventory-driven harness fails closed on duplicate identity, wrong lane/class, unknown workers, missing settlement, unsafe RunPod teardown, and cleanup failure while labeling mock evidence non-physical.
gates.command: python3 -m unittest -v scripts/test_fleet_proof.py && python3 scripts/fleet_proof.py run --inventory docs/fleet-proof/mock.inventory.json --mode mock

gates.acceptance: A canonical source deterministically emits Go, Rust, JSON, docs, and proof projections and excludes every non-production cell from its advertised projection.

Quote and submit reject unsupported exact job/model pairs before input writes; registration atomically materializes only current generated production cells with the matrix hash; legacy array-only workers are inert; and claim, count, planner, peer-routing, and scheduler-explain paths require an exact row rather than a Cartesian product.

The generated matrix is the enforced authority for database catalogue, worker registration/heartbeat advertisements, scheduler filters, agent runner dispatch, receipts, and documentation; unsupported or soak-only combinations cannot register or count as supply.
gates.next_action: Make the agent consume generated exact cells instead of hand-maintained roll-ups, derive dispatch resolver kind and immutable catalogue metadata from the matrix, bind receipts to cell/matrix/build/device identity, and add rollback plus negative runner tests.

gates.acceptance: A lane moves experimental -> soak_only -> production only through signed correctness, performance, fault, provenance, and soak bundles; rollback automatically removes its advertised supply and claims.
gates.next_action: Encode promotion states and required artifact hashes in the runtime source, add an atomic rollback path, and refuse manual status edits without a validated bundle.

gates.acceptance: At least one CUDA/PyTorch-or-container runtime demanded by target buyers completes ordinary register -> schedule -> run -> verify -> bill -> receipt flow without a separate demo-only control path.
gates.next_action: Interview the first wedge buyers to choose vLLM/PyTorch/container scope, implement one narrow production cell, and route it through the same runtime matrix and settlement path.

gates.acceptance: Docs, API, CLI, app, receipts, and marketing derive supported runtime/platform language from the production projection and never say all platforms, all runtimes, clustered, confidential, or production-ready outside proven cells.
gates.next_action: Generate a claim vocabulary artifact from the runtime matrix and add repository plus response-contract checks for forbidden overbroad language.

gates.acceptance: Two physical Apple machines with distinct device/worker identity complete distinct work, signed receipts, disconnect/rejoin, credential revocation, and replacement recovery; clustering is not inferred.
gates.next_action: When the Studio arrives, enroll both machines from a clean inventory, run the physical fleet harness, force disconnect/revocation/rejoin, and preserve device plus artifact identity.

gates.acceptance: Two or more independently addressed CUDA workers pass lane identity, distinct work, restart, OOM, corrupt output, settlement, artifact collection, spend cap, and verified teardown.
gates.next_action: After local lane promotion is ready and the user explicitly authorizes a dollar cap, provision short-lived RunPods from a pinned inventory and fail closed if spend or teardown cannot be verified.

gates.acceptance: Each production Metal and CUDA cell passes a 24-hour physical soak with declared load, no silent fallback, no capability overclaim, bounded error/restart rates, and complete cleanup/reconciliation.
gates.next_action: Promote only cells that have first passed short physical proofs, schedule the 24-hour run with cost/thermal limits, and archive raw liveness, fault, receipt, and cleanup evidence.

facets.definition_of_5: At least one artifact lane is an ordinary verified, billed product path with authoritative quality, held-out generalization, and repeatable external end-to-end advantage.

gates.acceptance: A transcode job type has a manifest, catalogue cell, scheduler capability, agent runner, deterministic artifact validation, receipt, billing, retry, and cleanup in the ordinary product path.
gates.next_action: Choose one codec/container/profile, define an ffprobe-plus-hash acceptance manifest, and implement one end-to-end lane before broadening formats.

gates.acceptance: Render acceptance binds reference-free anti-quality-shaving checks, deterministic scene/settings identity, authoritative delivery thresholds, and receipts that cannot promote synthetic or toy evidence.
gates.next_action: Freeze scene/settings/reference hashes, add perceptual plus structural anti-shortcut checks, and make authoritative thresholds fail closed in the ordinary receipt path.

gates.acceptance: Promotion succeeds on a preregistered held-out corpus spanning scene geometry, materials, lighting, motion, resolution, and device class; per-case failures remain visible and no tuned scene substitutes for generalization.
gates.next_action: Create immutable train/tune/held-out partitions, preregister thresholds before optimization, and run the canonical benchmark evaluator over every held-out case.

gates.acceptance: At least 20 external artifact jobs pass authoritative quality and achieve at least 1.5x net end-to-end speedup or a declared cost/privacy win against their pinned baseline.
gates.next_action: Recruit only after one lane is productized and held-out gates pass; freeze each buyer baseline and preserve delivered artifacts plus acceptance decisions.

facets.definition_of_5: A stranger can securely enroll, revoke, update, and uninstall a signed/notarized supplier app without a terminal, with real server exchange and clean-machine evidence.

gates.acceptance: The macOS app durably persists the exact canonical cxer2 pending origin, request id, and device fingerprint; rejects any cxeb2 approval that does not match that pending request before network use; creates a persistent non-exportable P-256 key; revalidates HTTPS; refuses authenticated redirects and cacheable secret responses; signs the origin/request-bound v2 server transcript; probes the returned bearer before Keychain persistence; rolls back partial or cancelled persistence; and gates launch on consent plus complete enrollment. It does not prove an account-facing approval UI, response-loss recovery, request-time device binding, server revocation from the app, or signed distribution.
gates.next_action: Run the persistent non-exportable key assertion inside an entitled signed app test host (the unsigned SwiftPM host skips it), archive a fresh release build, and keep the cross-origin substitution test non-skipped before promotion.

gates.acceptance: An authBuyer-only endpoint decodes the exact canonical cxer2 schema, verifies version/audience/P-256 key/request-id and a trusted configured HTTPS origin, derives account and supplier ownership only from authentication, persists that trusted origin/request binding on the ten-minute code row, and returns the byte-stable cxeb2 bundle under no-store. Client Host/X-Forwarded-Proto, extra identity fields, malformed/tampered requests, and cross-account ownership cannot alter issuance.

The server issues ten-minute account/audience/P-256-key/trusted-origin/request-id-bound single-use v2 codes, requires a device proof over that exact transcript during exchange, stores only secret hashes, returns no-store responses, serializes concurrent exchange/rotation/revocation, supersedes older same-source rotations across device keys, prevents pending-code resurrection and rotation downgrade, and exposes account-scoped audit/revocation. The resulting credential is enrollment-device-bound metadata but remains a bearer token at runtime.

A short-lived, audience-bound, single-use enrollment code is exchanged for a device-bound worker credential; replay, expiry, wrong account/device, interrupted exchange, rotation, and server revocation fail closed with auditable recovery.
gates.next_action: Bind regular worker requests to the enrolled private key or narrow the product contract to revocable bearer credentials, retire or explicitly isolate the legacy unbound token-mint route, and add response-loss/restart recovery before promoting the outcome.

gates.acceptance: First run performs account verification, one-time exchange, configuration, consent, authenticated health check, restart persistence, rotation, and revocation entirely in the app with actionable recovery.
gates.next_action: Build the account-facing approval UI over the authenticated cxer2-to-cxeb2 adapter, add response-loss/restart credential recovery plus in-app server rotation/revocation, then run the release app through the complete ceremony against a restarted local control server.

gates.acceptance: The app is Developer ID signed, hardened-runtime notarized and stapled, distributed over HTTPS, and updated through a signed feed with rollback and credential preservation.
gates.next_action: Obtain/use the user's Apple developer identity only with authorization, automate archive/sign/notarize/staple/feed verification, and test rollback on a disposable machine.

gates.acceptance: Ten clean machines install, enroll, contribute a verified task in under five minutes, update, revoke, and uninstall; uninstall removes service/config/credentials and every intervention is recorded.
gates.next_action: After signed distribution, run a scripted clean-machine study across supported macOS versions and archive screen/time/log evidence plus residue scans.

facets.definition_of_5: Published Python, JavaScript, and CLI surfaces install by pinned version, state unsupported sync/stream behavior explicitly, pass complete contracts, and let unfamiliar developers reach a real result quickly.

gates.acceptance: The Python SDK is a PEP 517 package that builds, installs in a clean virtualenv, imports outside the checkout, and passes installed-package tests without PYTHONPATH tricks.
The CLI builds checksummed macOS/Linux archives with embedded version, commit, date, toolchain, platform, and modified state, then installs and smokes from a clean directory.
Signed, versioned wheel and sdist artifacts are published to the chosen package index and install by pinned version on every declared Python release with provenance verification.
gates.next_action: Choose the package name/index, add trusted-publishing provenance and a version matrix, reserve publication until the API contract and supported runtime claims are accurate.

gates.acceptance: Versioned signed CLI artifacts and a Homebrew formula install by pinned version on supported macOS/Linux targets and expose the immutable build identity.
gates.next_action: Add signing/attestation and formula generation to the verified local build, then publish only from a clean tagged CI workflow.

gates.acceptance: A canonical source deterministically inventories every registered Go route and auth kind, every public Python method and CLI command with its exact route use, explicit async/sync/token-stream/artifact-stream behavior, unsupported OpenAI calls, and every accepted OpenAI model name against an exact production runtime cell; adversarial tests reject missing routes, ambiguity, source drift, and overbroad compatibility.
gates.command: python3 scripts/api_contract.py --check --artifact-dir .artifacts/5x5/api-contract && python3 -m unittest -v scripts/test_api_contract.py && (cd control && GOCACHE="$PWD/../.artifacts/go-cache" go test . -run '^(TestAdminLogoutIsPublicAndIdempotentWithoutSessionCookie|TestOpenAIBatch(ModelNamesAcceptOnlyNativeIdentityOrOmittedDefault|RejectsBrandedCrossModelAliasesWithNativeHint))$' -count=1)

gates.acceptance: Python, JavaScript, and CLI contracts cover every advertised operation; batch-only versus synchronous/streaming behavior is typed and explicit; unsupported calls fail clearly; OpenAI-style aliases never imply model/runtime compatibility that the matrix cannot prove.
gates.next_action: Add one black-box network contract per currently static-only Python method and CLI command, cover chat/file/output plus typed branded-name rejection over HTTP, then scaffold the JavaScript client from the frozen contract.

gates.acceptance: A typed JavaScript/TypeScript client builds, contracts against the real API, publishes by pinned version with provenance, and documents the same support boundaries as Python and CLI.
gates.next_action: Scaffold the minimal generated/typed client only after the API operation matrix is frozen, then add clean npm-pack install and live contract tests.

gates.acceptance: Five developers unfamiliar with the repository install a published client and reach a real supported result in under ten minutes without private-repo access or operator repair.
gates.next_action: Run the study after package/CLI distribution and buyer activation are live; record clock, errors, interventions, comprehension, and successful receipt.

facets.definition_of_5: Versioned project policies match implemented behavior, all claims are mechanically evidence-bound, naming and confidential-compute boundaries are screened, launch-critical external advice is obtained, and a validated wedge produces repeat paid use.

gates.acceptance: Project policies are authored, reviewed, and approved in a separately authorized policy pass without being treated as engineering or launch evidence.
gates.next_action: Keep policy and terms work outside the current engineering release; authorize a separate pass only when the business owner is ready to make the required decisions.

gates.acceptance: Any future policy set has an independently runnable governance check, but that work is not coupled to or claimed by this engineering release.
gates.next_action: Define policy governance only as part of a separately authorized policy pass; do not require unstaged policy files for engineering proof.

gates.acceptance: A machine-readable policy scans active outward-facing documentation and public HTML for stale proof counts, whole-product proof, decentralized/universal-runtime, confidential/air-gap, full OpenAI compatibility, legacy split, and only-stub overclaims; required topology, runtime, money, API, privacy, distribution, simulation, and archival boundaries are present, the site build cannot stamp a test count, and adversarial rejection tests pass.
gates.command: node scripts/site-build.mjs && python3 scripts/validate_claims.py && python3 -m unittest -v scripts/test_validate_claims.py

gates.acceptance: Project-owned contacts, retention periods, response targets, deletion steps, incident ownership, version acceptance, and earnings-risk disclosures are resolved and every operational promise has a passing implementation/drill test.
gates.next_action: Resolve the decision ledger with the user, wire retention/deletion/incident controls to product code and runbooks, then remove placeholders only when the corresponding tests pass.

gates.acceptance: A candidate name is applied consistently only after documented domain, app/package marketplace, corporate registry, search-engine, social, and relevant trademark-database screening; the project never calls this legal clearance without counsel.
gates.next_action: The dated knockout screen found a direct same-sector Compute Exchange collision. Generate distinctive candidates, complete package/domain/corporate/CIPO/USPTO/WIPO searches with saved results, and let the user choose before renaming; do not call this legal clearance.

gates.acceptance: Every quantitative or capability claim in docs, API, CLI, app, site, release notes, and receipts maps to a current proven gate/artifact; stale, modeled, synthetic, external-pending, and refuted evidence cannot render as an unqualified claim.
gates.next_action: Extend the proven high-risk phrase/boundary guard into a complete claim registry keyed to gate and artifact hashes, then scan API/CLI/app/site/release outputs and fail CI when a claim lacks an allowed evidence state and qualifier.

gates.acceptance: Private-pool and encrypted transport/storage guarantees are stated exactly; CX never claims TEE, operator blindness, air-gapping, confidential compute, or data sovereignty without corresponding enforced and independently attested controls.
gates.next_action: Inventory security/privacy phrases, map each to code and threat model, add forbidden-claim tests, and publish an explicit non-guarantees section beside private-pool pricing.

gates.acceptance: Before public paid launch, jurisdiction-specific professional advice addresses payments/MSB, tax reporting, privacy, contractor status, sanctions, and consumer/commercial terms; required controls are implemented.
gates.next_action: Use the project-authored policies and data/payment diagrams to scope counsel/accounting questions when launch jurisdiction and business entity are chosen; do not self-close this gate.

gates.acceptance: At least five target buyers independently confirm the same urgent privacy, memory-capacity, verification, or outcome-pricing problem and three complete a paid pilot against a declared alternative.
gates.next_action: After one supported workload and honest comparison exist, run structured interviews/pilots with preregistered disconfirming questions rather than pitching the whole marketplace vision.

gates.acceptance: At least three unaffiliated buyers return for a second paid, completed, reconciled job without operator subsidy or a test-only incentive.
gates.next_action: Measure cohort return only after live money and wedge pilots; exclude internal accounts, free credits, failed settlement, and manually executed jobs.

gates.acceptance: Any claim that marketplace data is a moat is backed by a preregistered held-out prediction/routing/pricing improvement over a no-market-data baseline and remains privacy-policy compliant; otherwise no such moat is claimed.
gates.next_action: Keep the claim disabled until a real cohort exists, then define a privacy-safe held-out experiment before inspecting results.

gates.acceptance: By explicit priority this is later: the deployed funnel links working signup/install, current policies, immutable builds, a real demo/receipt, support/status, and evidence-qualified claims with conversion telemetry.
gates.next_action: Defer implementation until distribution, policies, one wedge, and build provenance are ready; then build the smallest funnel whose claims are generated from the claim registry.

facets.definition_of_5: Immutable deployed artifacts, independent failure domains, tested restore/failover and incident response, and 30-day SLO evidence make production operation reproducible and survivable.

gates.acceptance: The control binary exposes version, commit, build date, toolchain, target platform, and modified state at a build endpoint with injected-release and development-build tests.

Images and client artifacts are built from a clean commit, SBOM/provenance-attested, digest-pinned, expose matching build identity, and deploy by immutable artifact rather than mutable checkout state.
gates.next_action: Pin base image digests, add clean-CI build attestations/SBOMs and identity comparison, then make deploy accept only an approved digest.

gates.acceptance: Control, database, object data, secrets, and backups survive loss of one host/zone/account boundary without manual data reconstruction or duplicate money work.
gates.next_action: Design the smallest two-domain production topology and provider-independent backups, then run a destructive failover only after costs and data handling are approved.

gates.acceptance: A timed, artifacted restore/failover drill meets declared RPO/RTO and verifies application-level job, object, verdict, credential, and ledger integrity plus idempotent resumed work.
gates.next_action: Declare RPO/RTO, generate a representative encrypted backup fixture, restore into an isolated stack, run integrity queries, and resume pending outbox work exactly once.

gates.acceptance: Injected availability, queue, object-store, settlement, payout, credential, and data-deletion failures trigger owned alerts; the incident runbook records acknowledge, contain, recover, notify, and postmortem times against policy targets.
gates.next_action: Map each policy incident class to a metric/alert and owner, install a local notification sink first, then run game days against the deployed staging stack.

gates.acceptance: Thirty days of independently queryable telemetry meets the declared availability, dispatch, result, settlement, and recovery objectives with maintenance/exclusion rules frozen in advance.
gates.next_action: Define SLIs and burn alerts before launch, retain raw telemetry outside the app host, and start the 30-day clock only after immutable deployment and failover drills pass.
